answer:
There are 12 face cards in a standard 52-card deck, so the probability of drawing a face card first is 12/52. After removing one face card, there are 4 aces left among 51 cards, so the probability of drawing an ace second is 4/51. Multiplying gives (12/52) × (4/51) = 48/2652, which simplifies to 4/221.

\boxed{J}
